description: list_element_type (types/mod.rs descr-level) returns none for a list conjunction with no positive elem constraint, conflating 'unconstrained' with 'exact empty list'. any.lists = [Conj::top()] (pos empty), so list_element_type(any) = none — manufacturing the empty type from any. Under a root's earned-any input, SplitList binds head := none, the downstream call (h + h in fixtures2/00477) is dead-dropped as empty-argument, no CallSiteSummary is published, yet the entry stays in reachable_entries — MaterializeRoot then fatals with 'incomplete call edges'. Fix per-conjunction: positive sig with elem None marks exact-empty (conj contributes NO head — skip); elem-ful sigs intersect; a conj with no positive constraint contributes any. Preserves: elem(empty_list)=none, elem(list(int))=int. Add the conformance test list_element_type_of_any_is_any next to the existing macro cases in types_test.rs. Fixes guard_and_binding_reuse_single_list_head_extraction.
title: types: list head projection of an unconstrained list conjunction is any, not none
